title: Statistical Inference Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Hypothesis Testing, P-Values, and A/B Testing.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of 'Statistical Inference'?

options.text: To compress image datasets efficiently without losing quality.
To draw valid mathematical conclusions about a massive 'Population' by analyzing a small 'Sample' of data.
To query databases using raw SQL.
To ensure neural networks never crash.

questions.question: In the context of A/B Testing an ML model, what is the 'Null Hypothesis'?

options.text: The baseline assumption that your brilliant new ML model had absolutely no effect, and any observed improvement was just random chance.
The assumption that the model is perfectly accurate.
A hypothesis that predicts values equal to zero.
The assumption that the Python code contains a bug.

questions.question: If you calculate a 'P-Value' of 0.01 (1%) after running a model optimization test, what conclusion do you draw?

options.text: The model's accuracy is only 1%.
The results are statistically significant, meaning there is less than a 5% chance the observed improvement was due to random luck. You confidently deploy.
The results are 'Not Statistically Significant'. The improvement was random noise.
The test needs to run for 10 more years before concluding.

questions.question: Which term describes a range of values (e.g., $400k to $450k) that mathematically quantifies uncertainty, rather than predicting a single exact number?

options.text: Standard Deviation
A Point Estimate
A Confidence Interval
A Correlation Coefficient

questions.question: Why do tech giants like Netflix use A/B Testing to evaluate new machine learning algorithms?

options.text: Because Python code cannot be tested locally.
A/B Testing acts as a controlled scientific experiment in production, isolating the exact impact of the new ML model vs the old model on actual user behavior.
It splits cloud computing costs in half.
Because they are legally required to do so by the government.
